content: We can reframe the linguistic learning definition of reflection (as a vocabulary term describing the meta-cognitive act of thinking about one’s own thinking) as an implicit early-stage Self-Refine framework for human cognitive learning, where human learners iteratively refine their understanding of new concepts by reflecting on mismatches between their initial guesses of a word’s meaning and its formal dictionary definition or contextual usage. This framing reverses the common flow of AI research translating human cognition to AI algorithms, suggesting we can borrow structured human language learning reflection routines to improve LLM self-refinement pipelines.
